title: Dense Bi-Encoder Retrieval and Domain Transfer · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers dense bi-encoder retrieval and domain transfer into a new dense retrieval candidate service. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of dense retrieval candidate service with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.
Apply model-specific query and document prompts, prefixes, normalization, and maximum-length behavior.
Tune candidate depth from recall and downstream reranking evidence rather than context-window size.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Biencoder Offline Online
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 4055 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dense retrieval on one embedding space, candidate recall, domain, and language evidence; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate offline document encoding from online query encoding while preserving one versioned bi-encoder space.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Biencoder Offline Online.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Embedding Migration
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-embedding-migration-170erj" that uses case 8942 to gate dense retrieval on one embedding space, candidate recall, domain, and language evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Embedding Migration.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Embedding Migration into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Embedding Migration, keep Rag Embedding Migration connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-embedding-migration-170erj
# Competency: rag-embedding-migration.
# Case 8942: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_embedding_migration_170erj(query_model: str = "retriever-v4", document_model: str = "retriever-v4", candidate_count: int = 50, relevant_rank: int = 4, domain_evaluated: bool = True, multilingual_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_170erj = "170erj-jre071-8942"
    assert evidence_variant_170erj
    evidence_axes_170erj = (
        "corpus170erj",
        "queryjre071",
        "tenant8942",
        "index170erj8942",
        "promptjre0718942",
        "evaluation8942170erj",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_170erj)) == 6
    if query_model != document_model:
        return False, 0, "model-space-mismatch"
    if candidate_count <= 0 or relevant_rank < 1 or relevant_rank > candidate_count:
        return False, relevant_rank, "candidate-rank"
    if not domain_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "domain-transfer"
    if not multilingual_evaluated:
        return False, relevant_rank, "language-transfer"
    recall_proxy = candidate_count - relevant_rank + 1
    assert recall_proxy > 0
    return True, recall_proxy, "dense-candidates"
